# pydiylc

Python emitter for DIYLC (DIY Layout Creator) `.diy` circuit-layout files. Write layouts in Python, open them in DIYLC. This document is the AI-friendly entry point — everything an assistant needs to use the library without browsing the repo.

## What this library is

- Source of truth: the Java app at https://github.com/bancika/diy-layout-creator
- pydiylc writes the same XML format DIYLC writes natively. Target: DIYLC 5.x (file format compatible back to v3).
- Coordinates are floats in **inches** by default. The DIYLC editor grid is **0.1 in**. Round positions to multiples of 0.1 unless you have a reason not to.
- Sizes (length, width, pin spacing, board spacing, hole size, etc.) are `Measure(value, unit)`. Helper constructors: `inches(v)`, `mm(v)`, `cm(v)`.

## Minimal example

```python
from pydiylc import Project, PerfBoard, Resistor, SolderPad, CopperTrace

p = Project(title="Booster", width_cm=10, height_cm=8)
p.add(PerfBoard("Board1", x1=1.0, y1=1.0, x2=3.0, y2=2.5))
p.add(SolderPad("P1", x=1.2, y=1.2))
p.add(SolderPad("P2", x=1.2, y=1.8))
p.add(Resistor("R1", x1=1.2, y1=1.2, x2=1.2, y2=1.8, value="10K"))
p.add(CopperTrace("T1", points=[(1.2, 1.8), (2.0, 1.8)]))
p.save("booster.diy")
```

## Project

```python
Project(
    title: str = "New Project",
    author: str = "",
    width_cm: float = 29.0,
    height_cm: float = 21.0,
    grid_inches: float = 0.1,
    dot_spacing: int = 1,
    file_version: tuple[int, int, int] = (5, 7, 0),
)
```

`p.add(component)` appends; `p.save(path)` writes XML.

## Components

All component constructors are dataclasses — every field has a sensible default except positional anchors (`name`, coordinates).

| Python | DIYLC class | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `BlankBoard` | `diylc.boards.BlankBoard` | Plain rectangular board |
| `PerfBoard` | `diylc.boards.PerfBoard` | Perfboard with through-hole grid |
| `VeroBoard` | `diylc.boards.VeroBoard` | Stripboard (continuous copper strips) |
| `Resistor` | `diylc.passive.Resistor` | Through-hole resistor (two-pin) |
| `RadialFilmCapacitor` | `diylc.passive.RadialFilmCapacitor` | Film cap (two-pin, default unit `nF`) |
| `RadialCeramicDiskCapacitor` | `diylc.passive.RadialCeramicDiskCapacitor` | Ceramic disk cap (two-pin, default unit `pF`) |
| `RadialElectrolytic` | `diylc.passive.RadialElectrolytic` | Polarized electrolytic (two-pin, default unit `uF`) |
| `PotentiometerPanel` | `diylc.passive.PotentiometerPanel` | 3-lug panel-mount pot |
| `DiodePlastic` | `diylc.semiconductors.DiodePlastic` | Plastic diode (e.g. 1N4148) |
| `LED` | `diylc.semiconductors.LED` | Through-hole LED |
| `TransistorTO92` | `diylc.semiconductors.TransistorTO92` | TO-92 3-pin transistor |
| `DIL_IC` | `diylc.semiconductors.DIL_IC` | DIP IC (4–50 pin) |
| `CopperTrace` | `diylc.connectivity.CopperTrace` | Polyline trace |
| `Jumper` | `diylc.connectivity.Jumper` | Insulated jumper wire (two-pin) |
| `HookupWire` | `diylc.connectivity.HookupWire` | Curved insulated wire (2 or 4 control points) |
| `SolderPad` | `diylc.connectivity.SolderPad` | Single pad / drilled hole |
| `TraceCut` | `diylc.connectivity.TraceCut` | Breaks a stripboard strip at one hole |
| `MiniToggleSwitch` | `diylc.electromechanical.MiniToggleSwitch` | Toggle / 3PDT bypass foot switch |
| `PlasticDCJack` | `diylc.electromechanical.PlasticDCJack` | 2.1mm DC barrel (pedal power) |
| `OpenJack1_4` | `diylc.electromechanical.OpenJack1_4` | Open-frame 1/4" guitar jack |
| `Label` | `diylc.misc.Label` | Free-floating text |

For exact field lists, defaults, types, and allowed enum values, load **`catalog.json`** at repo root. It is the canonical machine-readable schema.

## JSON ingestion (preferred path for LLMs)

You don't have to write Python. Emit a JSON document and call `Project.from_dict(...)` or `Project.from_json(...)`:

```python
from pydiylc import Project

p = Project.from_dict({
    "title": "Booster",
    "width_cm": 10, "height_cm": 8,
    "components": [
        {"type": "PerfBoard", "name": "Board1", "x1": 1.0, "y1": 1.0, "x2": 3.0, "y2": 2.5},
        {"type": "TransistorTO92", "name": "Q1", "x": 1.5, "y": 1.3,
         "value": "2N5088", "pinout": "BJT_EBC"},
        {"type": "Resistor", "name": "R1", "x1": 1.5, "y1": 1.3,
         "x2": 1.5, "y2": 1.0, "value": "1M"},
        {"type": "DIL_IC", "name": "U1", "x": 2.0, "y": 1.5,
         "value": "TL072", "pin_count": "_8"},
        {"type": "PotentiometerPanel", "name": "VR1", "x": 1.0, "y": 4.0,
         "resistance": "100K", "taper": "LOG"}
    ]
})
p.save("booster.diy")
```

Each component dict needs a `type` field naming a pydiylc class. The rest are kwargs. Measure fields (`length`, `width`, `pin_spacing`, etc.) accept either `{"value": 0.1, "unit": "in"}` or a bare number (interpreted as inches).

## Value strings

Two-pin components parse their `value` string as `number + unit`:

- Resistor: `"4.7K"`, `"1M"`, `"470R"`. Bare number → `K`.
- Film cap: `"100nF"`, `"1uF"`. Bare → `nF`.
- Ceramic cap: `"100pF"`, `"1000pF"`. Bare → `pF`.
- Electrolytic: `"22uF"`, `"470uF"`. Bare → `uF`.

## Enums — the values you must respect

(Subset; see `catalog.json` `enum_pool` for the full list.)

- `power` (Resistor): `QUARTER`, `HALF`, `ONE`, `TWO`
- `voltage` (Capacitors): `_4V`, `_6V3`, `_10V`, `_16V`, `_25V`, `_35V`, `_50V`, `_63V`, `_100V`, `_160V`, `_200V`, `_250V`, `_400V`, `_450V`, `_600V`, `_1kV`
- `display`: `NAME`, `VALUE`, `NONE`, `BOTH` (transistors also accept `PINOUT`)
- `orientation` (TransistorTO92, DIL_IC, PotentiometerPanel, Label): `DEFAULT`, `_90`, `_180`, `_270`
- `pinout` (TransistorTO92): `BJT_EBC`, `BJT_CBE`, `BJT_BCE`, `BJT_ECB`, `JFET_*`, `MOSFET_*`, `REGULATOR_*` — see catalog for the full list
- `pin_count` (DIL_IC): `_4`, `_6`, `_8`, `_10`, ... `_50` (even values only)
- `taper` (PotentiometerPanel): `LIN`, `LOG`, `REV_LOG`, `W`, `S`, `M`, `N`
- `view` (PotentiometerPanel): `ShaftDown`, `ShaftUp`
- `switch_type` (MiniToggleSwitch): `SPST`, `SPDT`, `SPDT_off`, `DPDT`, `DPDT_off`, `DPDT_ononon_1/2`, `_3PDT`, `_3PDT_off`, `_4PDT`, `_4PDT_off`, `_4PDT_ononon_1/2`, `_5PDT`, `_5PDT_off`. Use `_3PDT` for a true-bypass foot switch.
- `polarity` (PlasticDCJack): `NONE`, `CENTER_POSITIVE`, `CENTER_NEGATIVE` (most pedals are CENTER_NEGATIVE)
- `type` (OpenJack1_4): `MONO`, `STEREO`, `SWITCHED`
- `orientation` (VeroBoard, TraceCut, MiniToggleSwitch): `HORIZONTAL`, `VERTICAL`. For stripboard the trace cut orientation must match the parent board's.
- `gauge` (HookupWire AWG): `_12` ... `_30`
- `type` (SolderPad): `ROUND`, `SQUARE`, `OVAL_HORIZONTAL`, `OVAL_VERTICAL`
- `style` (Jumper, HookupWire): `SOLID`, `DASHED`, `DOTTED`
- `horizontal_alignment` (Label): `LEFT`, `CENTER`, `RIGHT`
- `vertical_alignment` (Label): `TOP`, `CENTER`, `BOTTOM`

All enum violations raise `ValueError` with the full allowed list in the message.

## Coordinate conventions

- Origin top-left.
- Y increases downward (matches DIYLC's editor).
- Two-pin components take `x1,y1,x2,y2`. The library inserts the midpoint automatically.
- `CopperTrace` takes a list of `(x, y)` tuples; ≥ 2 points required.
- `HookupWire` takes 2 endpoints (auto-interpolated to 4) or 4 explicit control points.

## Colors

Anywhere a color is accepted, pass a 6- or 3-char hex string (no `#`) or an int. Examples: `"ffe303"`, `"abc"`, `0xff0000`.

## Native SVG preview

```python
from pydiylc.svg import render_svg, render_svg_file

render_svg_file(project, "preview.svg")   # open in any browser
svg_text = render_svg(project)            # or get the string
```

This is a quick-feedback preview, not pixel-identical to DIYLC. For high-fidelity output, run DIYLC headless on the saved `.diy`:

```
diylc -convert layout.diy layout.png
```

## GTK4 viewer (`pydiylc-view`)

```bash
pydiylc-view path/to/layout.py     # opens a Wayland-native window
```

The viewer auto-reloads on file save (mtime poll), supports mouse drag to
pan, scroll to zoom, click to select. The script you point it at must
define either a top-level ``project = Project(...)`` or
``def build() -> Project``. Requires optional extras: install with
``pip install pydiylc[viewer]`` plus system GTK 4.

## Reading .diy files

```python
from pydiylc import Project, read_warnings

p = Project.read("some_pedal.diy")
print(p.title, len(p.components))
for w in read_warnings(p):
    print("⚠", w)  # unknown components, malformed fields
```

Reader handles modern (`<project>`) and v3 (`<org.diylc.core.Project>`) roots.
About 83% of components in real community layouts are recognized; unknown
types (e.g. tubes, axial caps, schematic symbols) are skipped with a warning
instead of failing the parse.

## What this library does NOT do

- v1 (legacy flat-format) `.diy` files are not parsed.
- Tubes, transformers, BJT/FET/IC schematic symbols, lever switches, rotary controls, and TO-220/TO-3 transistor packages are not yet implemented. They follow the same pattern when added.

## Where to look next

- `catalog.json` — full schema for codegen / validation
- `examples/demo_booster.py` — a complete working file
- `src/pydiylc/components.py` — every component's docstring + XML it emits
- `src/pydiylc/enums.py` — single source of truth for enum values
